pkg/pglockx.purpose: Thin constructor for cirello.io/pglock distributed lock clients backed by a PostgreSQL table (distributed_locks). Enforces the invariant that HeartbeatInterval must be less than half the LeaseTime to prevent lock expiry before renewal.

patterns.name: Validate config before constructing the client
patterns.description: Config.Validate() enforces LeaseTime >= 2*HeartbeatInterval and a non-empty Owner. Call Validate() or rely on New() which calls it internally — never pass an unvalidated Config to pglock.UnsafeNew.
patterns.example: if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, fmt.Errorf("invalid lock configuration: %w", err) }

patterns.name: Use DefaultHeartbeatInterval and DefaultLeaseTime
patterns.description: DefaultHeartbeatInterval (3s) and DefaultLeaseTime (1m) satisfy the heartbeat < lease/2 invariant. Prefer them when no specific timing is required.
patterns.example: cfg := pglockx.Config{LeaseTime: pglockx.DefaultLeaseTime, HeartbeatInterval: pglockx.DefaultHeartbeatInterval, Owner: "billing-worker"}

patterns.name: Fixed table name — never override per caller
patterns.description: The lock table is the hardcoded unexported constant lockTable ("distributed_locks"). All lock clients share this table — do not create per-feature lock tables.
patterns.example: pglock.WithCustomTable(lockTable) // internal; not configurable by callers

key_file_guides.role: Entire package — Config, Validate(), New() wrapping pglock.UnsafeNew with fixed table and configurable lease/heartbeat/owner.
key_file_guides.watch_for: pglock.UnsafeNew is used (not pglock.New) because the safe constructor requires a createTable migration conflicting with Atlas-managed schema. Do not switch to pglock.New without adding distributed_locks to Atlas migrations.

anti_patterns: Setting HeartbeatInterval >= LeaseTime/2 — Validate() rejects it; the heartbeat must renew the lease well before expiry.
Leaving Owner empty — the lock client requires an owner string to identify the holding process.
Creating per-feature lock tables by overriding WithCustomTable — all locks share the distributed_locks table.
Using pglock.New instead of pglock.UnsafeNew without adding the distributed_locks table to Atlas migrations.

decisions.rationale: pglock.New auto-creates the lock table at startup, conflicting with Atlas-managed migrations. UnsafeNew skips auto-create; the table is created via the Atlas pipeline.
A single shared table avoids schema proliferation and keeps the Atlas migration surface minimal; all lock use cases share the table with owner-scoped rows.

code_examples.scenario: Construct a pglock client for a billing worker process

pkg/pgxpoolobserver.purpose: Single-function OTel metrics registration for pgxpool.Pool, exposing 13 pool statistics as observable gauges/counters via one RegisterCallback call. Call ObservePoolMetrics once per pool at startup; the registered callback polls pool.Stat() on each OTel collection cycle.

patterns.name: Single RegisterCallback for all metrics
patterns.description: Collect all metric.Observable instances into allMetrics and pass them to one meter.RegisterCallback call. OTel requires all observables used inside a callback to be declared in the same RegisterCallback.
patterns.example: _, err = meter.RegisterCallback(func(_ context.Context, o metric.Observer) error { ... }, allMetrics...)

patterns.name: Append-then-register pattern
patterns.description: Each metric is created with meter.Int64ObservableCounter/Gauge, error-checked immediately (return on error), then appended to allMetrics before RegisterCallback. New metrics follow this exact sequence.
patterns.example: m, err := meter.Int64ObservableGauge("pgxpool.idle_conns", ...); if err != nil { return err }; allMetrics = append(allMetrics, m)

patterns.name: additionalAttributes variadic for label injection
patterns.description: Callers pass extra attribute.KeyValue pairs via the variadic additionalAttributes parameter; every ObserveInt64 call appends them via metric.WithAttributes(additionalAttributes...).

patterns.name: Guard derived metrics against division by zero
patterns.description: avgAcquiredDurationMetric is only observed when acquireCount > 0. Replicate this guard for any future ratio or average metric.

key_file_guides.role: Entire package; exposes only ObservePoolMetrics(meter, pool, ...attributes). 13 metrics registered in a single function.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Each of the 13 meter.Int64Observable* calls is error-checked and returns immediately on failure. Adding a metric requires: create → check error → append to allMetrics → observe inside the callback.

anti_patterns: Calling ObservePoolMetrics more than once for the same pool/meter pair — duplicate metric registration errors.
Adding a new metric without appending it to allMetrics — it will never be observed in the callback.
Omitting the division-by-zero guard on derived ratio metrics — produces NaN or panic in the callback.

decisions.decision: Observable push model (RegisterCallback) instead of explicit Record calls.
decisions.rationale: Pool stats are instantaneous gauge values; polling only when the OTel collector pulls avoids background goroutines and unnecessary polling when no collector is active.

pkg/redis.purpose: Thin factory layer for go-redis/v9 client construction from a Viper-backed Config struct, with built-in OTel tracing and metrics via redisotel. Supports standalone and Sentinel topologies and always enforces TLS 1.3 minimum when TLS is enabled.

patterns.name: Validate before NewClient
patterns.description: Config.Validate() uses errors.Join to accumulate field errors. Always call Validate() before constructing the client in Wire wiring or application startup.
patterns.example: if err := cfg.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Functional options for OTel providers
patterns.description: TracerProvider and MeterProvider are injected via Option funcs (WithTracingProvider, WithMeterProvider). Nil providers are silently ignored; options layer on top of the base Options struct.
patterns.example: client, err := redis.NewClient(redis.Options{Config: cfg}, redis.WithTracingProvider(tp), redis.WithMeterProvider(mp))

patterns.name: Configure sets Viper defaults with prefix
patterns.description: Call Configure(v, prefix) to register all Redis defaults under a namespaced Viper prefix. Never call viper.SetDefault inline in app config for Redis settings.

patterns.name: OTel instrumentation is always attempted
patterns.description: redisotel.InstrumentTracing and InstrumentMetrics are called unconditionally; when no provider is set the opts slice is empty and the calls are effectively no-ops. Do not short-circuit them.
patterns.example: if err := redisotel.InstrumentTracing(client, tracingOpts...); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: TLS minimum version is TLS 1.3
patterns.description: When TLS.Enabled is true, MinVersion is always tls.VersionTLS13. Do not override or relax this in callers.

key_file_guides.role: NewClient factory: applies functional options, builds TLS config, constructs standalone or Sentinel client, instruments tracing and metrics.
key_file_guides.watch_for: redisotel.InstrumentTracing/InstrumentMetrics are called even when providers are nil (empty opts slice). Do not add nil-guard short-circuits — the unconditional call is intentional.

key_file_guides.role: Config struct, Validate, Configure (Viper defaults), and Config.NewClient convenience method.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Config.NewClient() does not accept OTel options. Use NewClient(Options{Config: c}, WithTracingProvider(...)) directly when telemetry is needed.

anti_patterns: Constructing the go-redis client directly in application code — OTel instrumentation would be missing.
Calling Configure with an empty prefix — all keys collide at the root Viper namespace.
Ignoring Config.Validate() — an empty Address produces a client that fails at first use, not at construction.
Relaxing TLS MinVersion below tls.VersionTLS13 — a deliberate security invariant.

decisions.decision: OTel instrumentation is always attempted, not gated on provider presence.
decisions.rationale: redisotel calls are no-ops when no provider is passed, so always calling them ensures new callers automatically get observability when they wire providers, without conditional branching.

code_examples.scenario: Constructing a Redis client with OTel in Wire wiring

pkg/ref.purpose: Single-file utility providing IDOrKey — a discriminated union type holding either a ULID-format ID or an arbitrary string key, used wherever domain lookups must accept both identifiers interchangeably without ambiguity.

patterns.name: ULID-based discrimination via ParseIDOrKey
patterns.description: ParseIDOrKey uses ulid.Parse to decide which field to populate: successful parse → ID set, parse error → Key set. Never set both fields manually.
patterns.example: r := ref.ParseIDOrKey(rawParam) // r.ID set if ULID, r.Key set otherwise

patterns.name: Validate before service call
patterns.description: Always call Validate() before passing an IDOrKey into a service layer. It errors if both fields are empty (the all-zero case).
patterns.example: if err := r.Validate(); err != nil { return err }

key_file_guides.role: Defines IDOrKey with GetIDs, GetKeys, Validate, and ParseIDOrKey — the entire package surface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Validate only guards the all-empty case, not the both-set case. Callers must not set both ID and Key — semantics are undefined and unchecked.

anti_patterns: Setting both ID and Key on an IDOrKey struct — undefined semantics, no runtime guard.
Skipping Validate() before service calls — silently passes empty identifiers into domain logic.
Adding new identifier union types here — define domain-specific discriminated unions in the relevant domain package instead.

decisions.decision: Use ulid.Parse as the discriminator rather than a regex or length check.
decisions.rationale: ULID has a canonical 26-char Crockford Base32 format; a parse error cleanly distinguishes it from freeform key strings without false positives from length heuristics.

pkg/server.purpose: Shared HTTP server primitives — request attribute extraction, structured logging middleware, powered-by header middleware, and a MiddlewareFunc type alias — reused by both the v1 (openmeter/server) and v3 (api/v3/server) API servers with no business logic.

patterns.name: OTel semconv attribute keys
patterns.description: Use go.opentelemetry.io/otel/semconv/v1.27.0 key constants for all HTTP attribute names. Never hardcode string keys.

patterns.name: Chi middleware.LogFormatter / LogEntry interfaces
patterns.description: RequestLogger implements middleware.LogFormatter and RequestLoggerEntry implements middleware.LogEntry. Pass RequestLogger to middleware.RequestLogger() to integrate with Chi's logging chain.

patterns.name: Middleware factory pattern
patterns.description: Every middleware is exposed as a NewXxxMiddleware() returning func(http.Handler) http.Handler, matching Chi's middleware signature.

patterns.name: req_id depends on upstream RequestID middleware
patterns.description: GetRequestAttributes populates req_id only when middleware.RequestID is upstream in the chain. Absent middleware → absent attribute with no error.
patterns.example: if reqID := middleware.GetReqID(ctx); reqID != "" { attrs["req_id"] = reqID }

key_file_guides.role: Returns an OTel-keyed attribute map for a request. Requires middleware.RequestID upstream for the req_id field.
key_file_guides.watch_for: req_id is silently absent if RequestID middleware is missing; this is not an error.

key_file_guides.role: Structured slog-based request logger satisfying Chi's middleware.LogFormatter interface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Normal requests log at slog.LevelDebug; panics at slog.LevelError. Do not raise normal requests to LevelInfo without considering log volume.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the shared MiddlewareFunc type alias used across v1 and v3 server wiring.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must stay in sync with Chi's func(http.Handler) http.Handler signature. Never wrap in a struct.

key_file_guides.role: Injects the X-Powered-By header on every response.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Header value 'OpenMeter by Kong, Inc.' is asserted in poweredby_test.go — changing it breaks clients that parse this header.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic or domain package imports — this package must remain a pure infrastructure utility.
Hardcoding OTel attribute key strings instead of using semconv constants.
Returning errors from middleware constructors — they should panic on bad config or return a no-op handler.
Using GetRequestAttributes without middleware.RequestID in the chain — req_id will silently be missing.

decisions.decision: Log at DEBUG for normal requests, ERROR for panics.
decisions.rationale: High-volume request logs would flood INFO in production; DEBUG keeps them filterable without losing panic diagnostics.

code_examples.scenario: Add the powered-by middleware to a Chi router
